Patch Set 1: Code-Review+1

Can we do something like adding a flag to ext3_iget() that is allowing iget() for EA I idea?

That would preserve the sanity check, but allow scrub to work.

Patch Set 1:

> Patch Set 1: Code-Review+1
> 
> Can we do something like adding a flag to ext3_iget() that is allowing iget() for EA I idea?
> 
> That would preserve the sanity check, but allow scrub to work.

The existing EXT4_EA_INODE_FL does this, but when using it with iget, the ext4_iget will fail if the inode is not ea_inode.
and the problem for scrubbing is we don't know if the inode is ea_inode or not before iget, so we can't just use EXT4_EA_INODE_FL for every iget.

I guess we can add a new flag meaning don't do any checks just give me the inode?

Patch Set 1:

> > Patch Set 1: Code-Review+1
 > >
 > > Can we do something like adding a flag to ext3_iget() that is
 > allowing iget() for EA inodes?
 > >
 > > That would preserve the sanity check, but allow scrub to work.
 > 
 > The existing EXT4_EA_INODE_FL does this, but when using it with
 > iget, the ext4_iget will fail if the inode is not ea_inode.
 > and the problem for scrubbing is we don't know if the inode is
 > ea_inode or not before iget, so we can't just use EXT4_EA_INODE_FL
 > for every iget.
 > 
 > I guess we can add a new flag meaning don't do any checks just give
 > me the inode?

Yes, EXT4_IGET_EA_MAYBE or something. This might just ends up the same as "don't check anything", but would allow regular iget() to be checked against getting direct access to an EA inode.

Unfortunately yes, unless ext4 comes up with something that does scrubbing/scanning in kernel space like us using iget.

Patch Set 3:

Dongyang

The primary kernel patch series on master is RHEL 9.4. It has been discussed at the LWG and agreed that if people want to maintain other patch series due to their own interests then the onus is on them to monitor changes to the RHEL 9.4 patch series and make equivalent changes to their special interest patch series.

Peter

I think we just worked out exactly which ldiskfs series need this, we are just missing one for ubuntu20 and one for 6.10-ml. I'm going to include those for this instance, since this patch hasn't landed yet.

parents.subject: LU-6142 lnet: Fix style issues for socklnd_lib.c

commit.subject: LU-18080 ldiskfs: add NO_CHECKS flags for ext4_iget
commit.message:
LU-18080 ldiskfs: add NO_CHECKS flags for ext4_iget

Since v6.4-rc4-1-gb3e6bcb94590, the upstream kernel introduced
a strict checking of EXT4_IGET_EA_INODE and it has to match the
EXT4_EA_INODE_FL from the inode.

This creates a problem for osd scrub as we iterate over the inodes
according to bitmap we have no idea if the inode is an ea_inode or
not.

Add a new flag EXT4_IGET_NO_CHECKS and relax the strict checking
when the flag is used.

Cleanup osd_ldiskfs_iget_special(), the caller of osd_iget() now are
passing in the additional flags to ext4_iget().

Test-Parameters: trivial
Test-Parameters: fstype=ldiskfs serverdistro=el9.4 testlist=conf-sanity env=ONLY=32a
Signed-off-by: Li Dongyang <dongyangli@ddn.com>
Change-Id: I43c8da68b06c5592e197dd3b5e3f4f7c842cf7c4
Reviewed-on: https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/55896

Tested-by: Shuichi Ihara <sihara@ddn.com>
Tested-by: Maloo <maloo@whamcloud.com>
